description:
Vigilant's app container depends on the external Docker network named `web` (edge nginx at /opt/edge/ attaches here and upstreams to vigilant-app-1:8000). If the network doesn't exist, `docker compose up` fails with a confusing error mid-deploy. Add a preflight to `scripts/deploy.sh` that:

1. Verifies `docker network inspect web` succeeds — if not, abort with a clear "create with: docker network create web" message.
2. Verifies the compose file still declares the network as `external: true, name: web` (greppable check) — defensive against accidental edits.

Run the preflight BEFORE the git pull / image tagging steps so a missing network fails fast without disturbing rollback state.

NOTE: The original ticket description also mentioned "reload nginx by recreating the nginx container after any nginx/*.conf change (bind-mount inode trap)." That clause is obsolete — vigilant decoupled from nginx on 2026-05-14 (commit 9d1189d); shared proxy now at /opt/edge/. The inode-swap caveat now applies only to /opt/edge/'s own nginx, not this repo.
title: Preflight external `web` network check in deploy.sh
